Y.description: The quantity of paper rolls consumed every hour by a machine that operates on cash.

U.description: The lowest amount of individuals that need to be handled per hour.

A.description: The number of individuals processed by a cash-operated machine within an hour.

V.description: The highest quantity of paper rolls that can be utilized within a single hour

K.description: The number of individuals a card-only machine can handle in one hour.

W.description: Quantity of paper rolls utilized hourly by a machine that only accepts cards

r.description: The quantity of machines that exclusively accept cards

s.description: The quantity of machines that operate using cash.

zed.description: New variable representing the objective function

constraints.description: Constraint defining zed in terms of original variables.
The minimum number of individuals handled within an hour by machines accepting both cash and cards must be U.
The quantity of machines that only accept cards should not surpass the quantity of machines that accept cash.
The combined amount of paper rolls used per hour by machines that accept cash and those that only accept cards should not surpass V.
